zh.Broncroft Castle, Craven Arms, Shropshir: 英國史洛普爾克雷文 瓦爾特運動槍有限公司
zh.lifeSummary: 當Jane Burley於1385年出生在英國史洛普爾克雷文 瓦爾特運動槍有限公司時，她的父親Sir Roger Burley50歲，母親Lucy Gilford45歲。她於1401年與Thomas Corbet在英國斯塔福爾易亞斯特 斯塔福爾結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世在英國史洛普爾阿爾維勒。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Jane Burley于1385年出生在英国史洛普爾克雷文 瓦爾特運動槍有限公司时，她的父亲Sir Roger Burley50岁，母亲Lucy Gilford45岁。她于1401年与Thomas Corbet在英国斯塔福爾易亞斯特 斯塔福爾结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世在英国史洛普爾阿爾維勒。

nameMeanings.name: Burley
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of various places called Burley, for example in Hampshire, Rutland, Shropshire, and Yorkshire, from Old English burh ‘fortified manor, stronghold’ + lēah ‘woodland clearing’.
Americanized form of Swiss German Bürli: from a diminutive of būr ‘peasant, farmer’ (see Bauer ).

variants.name: Berley
meanings: English (Yorkshire): variant of   Burley  . This surname is now rare in Britain. It may also be a variant of   Birley  .
Americanized form of Swiss German Beerli, Berli, or Berly, and probably also of their South German cognate Behrle or of some other similar (like-sounding) surname. Compare Barley .

variants.name: Birley
meanings: English: habitational name from Birley in Ecclesfield (Yorkshire), East or North Bierley (Yorkshire), or Birley (Derbyshire).

variants.name: Burlew
meanings: Probably a variant of   Burley   1 or 2.

nameMeanings.name: Jane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: Elaborated spelling of   Jane  , a form popularized no doubt by the American actress  Jayne Mansfield  (  Vera Jayne Palmer ,  1933–67 ) and British ice-dancer  Jayne Torvill  (  b. 1957 ).

variants.name: Jain
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Jane Burley was born in 1385, in Craven Arms, Shropshire, England, her father, Sir Roger Burley, was 50 and her mother, Lucy Gilford, was 45. She married Thomas Corbet in 1401, in East Staffordshire, Staffordshire, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. She died in Alveley, Shropshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Jane Burley est née en 1385, à Craven Arms, Shropshire, Angleterre, son père, Sir Roger Burley, avait 50 ans et sa mère, Lucy Gilford, avait 45 ans. Elle a épousé Thomas Corbet en 1401, à East Staffordshire, Staffordshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée à Alveley, Shropshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Jane Burley 1385 in Craven Arms, Shropshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir Roger Burley 50 und ihre Mutter Lucy Gilford 45. Sie hat 1401 in East Staffordshire, Staffordshire, England Thomas Corbet geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb in Alveley, Shropshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Jane Burley è nata il nel 1385 nel Craven Arms, Shropshire, England, sua padre, Sir Roger Burley, aveva 50 anni e sua madre, Lucy Gilford, aveva 45 anni. Lei sposato Thomas Corbet nel 1401, nel East Staffordshire, Staffordshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 1 figlia. Morì nel Alveley, Shropshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Jane Burleyが1385年にイギリスシュロプシュアイアクラベン アルムズで生まれた時，父Sir Roger Burleyは50歳，母Lucy Gilfordは45歳であった。1401年にイギリススタフォードシュアイアエアスト スタフォードシュアイアでThomas Corbetと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女はイギリスシュロプシュアイアアルベリーで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Jane Burley(이)가 1385년 영국 슈럽실르 크레이븐 아스에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Roger Burley(은)는 50세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Lucy Gilford(은)는 45세였다. 그녀는 1401년 영국 스태퍼드실르 이스트 스태퍼드실르에서 Thomas Corbet(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 영국 슈럽실르 앨벨리에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Jane Burley nasceu em 1385, em Craven Arms, Shropshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Roger Burley, tinha 50 anos e sua mãe, Lucy Gilford, tinha 45 anos. Ela casou-se com Thomas Corbet em 1401, em East Staffordshire, Staffordshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 1 filha. Ela faleceu em Alveley, Shropshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Jane Burley родилась в 1385 г. в местности кравен армс, шропшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir Roger Burley было 50 лет, а матери, которую звали Lucy Gilford, 45. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Thomas Corbet в 1401 г. в местности эаст стаффордшир, стаффордшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь. Она умерла в местности алвели, шропшир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Jane Burley nació en 1385, en Craven Arms, Shropshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir Roger Burley, tenía 50 años y su mamá, Lucy Gilford, tenía 45 años. Se casó con Thomas Corbet en 1401, en East Staffordshire, Staffordshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 1 hija. Murió en Alveley, Shropshire, Inglaterra.
